examples: Halley was aware that the AU was one of the most fundamental of all astronomical measurements.
Halley realised that Mercury was so far away that its parallax angle would be very difficult to determine.
In November 1677, Halley observed a transit of the innermost planet, Mercury, from the desolate island of St Helena in the South Pacific.
